segments.caption: The video opens with José Mourinho at a press conference, dressed in a dark suit and light blue shirt, speaking into microphones with a serious expression. On-screen text highlights his bold statements: 'I'M SORRY I'M A BIT ARROGANT, WE HAVE A TOP MANAGER,' followed by 'AGAIN, PLEASE DON'T CALL ME ARROGANT BECAUSE WHAT I'M SAYING IS TRUE,' and 'I'M EUROPEAN CHAMPIONS, I AM NOT ONE OF THE BOTTOM.' The background features sponsor logos including 'Fly Emirates,' 'Barclaycard,' and 'Umbro.' The scene transitions to a montage of Mourinho in various confrontational moments—arguing with officials, clashing with other managers, and walking off the pitch—accompanied by bold text overlays like 'BIGGEST EGO' and 'THE STORY OF JOSÉ MOURINHO AGAINST THE WORLD.' The video then shifts to a narrator in a studio setting, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in front of a backdrop with football jerseys and a world map. He introduces 'The Beginnings: FC Porto,' with archival photos of a young Mourinho in a black-and-white striped jersey, part of a youth team. The narrator gestures as he speaks, and the screen displays text such as '300 GAMES' and 'FÉLIX MOURINHO,' while a red circle highlights the young Mourinho in the team photo. The segment ends with a close-up of a young Mourinho at a press conference, looking intense, with the word 'CRUEL' overlaid in bold, stylized text.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Narrator in a studio setting, standing before a backdrop adorned with football jerseys, a world map, and decorative plants, delivering a story about José Mourinho’s early career. The scene transitions to vintage footage of a young Mourinho in a black-and-white striped FC Porto youth jersey, highlighted with a red circle, emphasizing his early involvement with the club. The narrative then shifts to archival footage of Bobby Robson, shown in a dark suit and tie, speaking at a press conference with the Newcastle United crest behind him, where he praises Mourinho’s analytical skills and reports. Text overlays quote Robson: 'He’d come back and hand me a dossier that was absolutely first class. I mean: first class. As good as anything I’ve ever received.' and 'Here he was, in his early thirties, never been a player, never been a coach to speak of either, giving me reports as good as anything I ever got.' The video cuts back to the Narrator, who continues to explain Mourinho’s rise, followed by a montage of Mourinho in various roles—first in a dark tracksuit walking confidently on a pitch, then in a suit at a press conference with microphones, and finally in a white shirt with blue stripes, gesturing during a match. The segment concludes with the Narrator summarizing Mourinho’s early achievements: '2 Portuguese Championships, 3 Super Cups, 1 Portuguese Cup,' displayed as bold text on screen. The atmosphere is informative and nostalgic, blending personal storytelling with historical footage to trace Mourinho’s formative years under Robson’s mentorship.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dramatic, fiery visual effect, transitioning to a scene at a football match where José Mourinho, in a dark suit, is seen walking on the pitch with a stern expression, while Eva Carneiro, in a blue Chelsea kit, runs toward a player. The camera follows Mourinho as he walks away from the sideline, visibly upset, with the stadium crowd in the background. The scene cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, now in a black and blue Chelsea tracksuit, speaks into a microphone with a serious demeanor, his face showing frustration. On-screen text overlays quote his criticism of the medical staff, stating he was unhappy with their decision to send Carneiro onto the pitch, claiming the player only had a knock and was tired, and that the medical department left him with insufficient players during a counter-attack. The video then shifts to the Narrator in a studio with football memorabilia, explaining the aftermath: the players publicly backed Eva, and the club took the matter to court. The Narrator continues, describing Mourinho’s controversial use of the phrase 'filho da p*ta,' which he defends as a common football swear word without sexist connotation. The scene returns to Mourinho at the press conference, where he elaborates on his perspective, emphasizing the context of football language. The video ends with the Narrator summarizing the incident, highlighting the public fallout and legal action taken by the club.

segments.caption: The video opens with José Mourinho at a press conference, wearing a red Manchester United tracksuit with 'JM' on the chest, seated before microphones branded with MUTV and the club crest, against a backdrop featuring Manchester United and Adidas logos. He speaks with a serious expression, and on-screen text quotes him: 'I WANT HIM TO PLAY WELL, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE TEAM, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE FANS AND THAT’S WHAT IT IS.' The scene cuts to the Narrator in his studio, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in front of a world map and hanging football jerseys, gesturing as he speaks. The video then transitions to Paul Pogba at a press conference, wearing a grey sleeveless Manchester United training top, speaking into a microphone with a backdrop of sponsors including Samsung, Heineken, and Hard Rock Hotel & Casino. On-screen text quotes Pogba: 'IT STARTED WHEN I GOT INJURED. I SAID I WAS GOING TO MIAMI TO DO REHABILITATION. A PAPARAZZI TOOK A PHOTO OF ZULAY AND ME. JOSE SENT THE PICTURE TO MINO RAIOLA.' He continues, 'I DIDN’T LIKE THAT AT ALL. HE SAID ‘HE’S ON HOLIDAY WHEN WE ARE WORKING LIKE BASTARDS’. I TOLD MOURINHO, ‘ARE YOU SERIOUS? I’M INJURED. I TRAIN THREE TIMES A DAY HERE. WHO DO YOU THINK I AM?’' The scene briefly cuts to a street view with cars, including a red Mini Cooper, before returning to the Narrator. segments.caption: The video opens with José Mourinho at a press conference, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated before a backdrop featuring Manchester United and Adidas logos. The video then transitions to Mourinho in a purple puffer jacket on a training ground, walking among players in similar purple gear, with text overlays quoting his intense motivational speech: 'I FEEL THAT YOU ARE, HONESTLY, A VERY NICE GROUP OF GUYS. BUT, FOR 90 MINUTES, YOU CANNOT BE NICE. FOR 90 MINUTES, WE HAVE TO BE A BUNCH OF C*NTS, INTELLIGENT C*NTS, NOT STUPID C*NTS. B*STARDS IN THE SENSE OF YOU ARE GOING TO WIN MATCHES.' The scene shifts back to the Narrator, who continues his commentary, followed by a shot of Mourinho at a Tottenham Hotspur press event, standing before a backdrop with the club’s crest and AIA and Nike logos, wearing a purple jacket under a black vest, looking down with a serious expression.

segments.caption: The video opens with José Mourinho, wearing a purple puffer jacket over a black vest, walking on a Tottenham Hotspur training ground, interacting with players in grey and purple training gear. The scene cuts to a studio where the Narrator, in a brown t-shirt, speaks directly to the camera, gesturing expressively against a backdrop of a world map and hanging football jerseys. The video then transitions to training footage of Tottenham players in grey and pink kits, with on-screen text stating 'SIMPLY COULDN’T CHANGE THE SPURS CONSTANT IRREGULARITY'. A press conference clip shows Dele Alli in a white and grey Tottenham jersey, seated before a backdrop of sponsor logos including Nike and Vauxhall, with the name 'DELE ALLI' overlaid. The video returns to Mourinho on the training ground, now in a black puffer jacket, observing players, with a quote from him displayed: 'I SAW YOU DO INCREDIBLE MATCHES AND INCREDIBLE THINGS, BUT I ALWAYS FELT THAT YOU HAD UPS AND DOWNS. THERE IS A HUGE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN A PLAYER THAT KEEPS CONSISTENCY AND A PLAYER THAT HAS MOMENTS, AND THAT IS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN A TOP, TOP PLAYER AND A PLAYER WITH TOP, TOP POTENTIAL.' The Narrator reappears, emphasizing the theme with text overlays like 'PERFECTLY FIT MOURINHO’S TOTTENHAM' and 'ALTERNATED FANTASTIC GAMES AND INCREDIBLY POOR PERFORMANCES'. The segment ends with a close-up of a player in a grey Tottenham training kit, with the text 'DELE ALLI' again appearing, reinforcing the focus on the player’s inconsistent form under Mourinho’s management.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio adorned with a world map, hanging football jerseys, and decorative plants. He speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, introducing the segment titled 'Real Madrid: The Special One' under the 'Soccer Stories' brand. The scene cuts to archival footage of a football team in blue-and-white striped jerseys celebrating a trophy victory, with one player lifting a large silver cup amidst a crowd of jubilant fans. A split-screen appears, showing a man with a tattoo of a trophy on his arm on the left, and illustrations of three major football trophies—the UEFA Champions League, UEFA Europa League, and FIFA Club World Cup—on the right. The scene transitions to a press conference where José Mourinho, wearing a purple tracksuit, stands before a backdrop with 'I AM JOSÉ' printed on red chairs, addressing a room full of reporters. The final frames show a close-up of Mourinho in a grey tracksuit, looking intense, followed by a title card with bold white text 'I’M A SPECIAL ONE' over a red and black background with the 'Soccer Stories' logo. The segment ends with a countdown from 3 to 1, leading into the next part of the video.
